headlines.title: Medtronic Earnings Preview: What to Expect
headlines.summary: : Medtronic is set to report Q1 earnings, with analysts expecting high single-digit year-over-year EPS growth.

headlines.title: [Latest] Global Implantable Cardiac Fluorine-Free Resins Market Size/Share Worth USD 4.26 Billion by 2035 at a 10.8% CAGR: Healthcare Foresights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth Rate,SWOT Analysis)
headlines.summary: [220+ Pages Latest Report] According to a market research study published by Healthcare Foresights, the demand analysis of Global Implantable Cardiac Fluorine-Free Resins Market size & share revenue was valued at approximately USD 1.52 Billion in 2025 and is expected to reach USD 1.67 Billion in 2026 and is expected to reach around USD 4.26 Billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 10.8% between 2026 and 2035.

headlines.title: ISRG Growth Story Hinges on Da Vinci 5, Ion and Recurring Revenue
headlines.summary: Intuitive Surgical's growth rests on da Vinci 5, Ion, SP and digital tools as recurring revenue rises and procedure risks persist.

headlines.title: ISRG Trends Point to AI, Lung Biopsy Growth and Margin Pressure
headlines.summary: ISRG's growth is fueled by robotic surgery, Ion lung biopsy and AI tools, while tariffs, product mix and softer demand pressure margins.

headlines.title: Is ISRG Stock Worth Buying as Growth Stays Strong but Risks Rise
headlines.summary: ISRG's strong procedure growth, recurring revenue and cash position support its case, but tariffs, mix shifts and placement risks cloud the outlook.

description: Medtronic plc develops, manufactures, and sells device-based medical therapies to healthcare systems, physicians, clinicians, and patients in the United States, Ireland, and internationally. It operates through three segments: The Cardiovascular Portfolio, Neuroscience Portfolio, and Medical Surgical Portfolio. The Cardiovascular Portfolio segment offers implantable cardiac pacemakers, cardioverter defibrillators, and cardiac resynchronization therapy devices; cardiac ablation products; insertable cardiac monitor systems; TYRX products; and remote monitoring and patient-centered software. It also provides aortic valves, surgical valve replacement and repair products, endovascular stent grafts and accessories, and transcatheter pulmonary valves, left atrial appendage exclusion systems, extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) systems and percutaneous coronary intervention products, percutaneous angioplasty balloons, and endovenous products. The Neuroscience Portfolio segment offers medical devices and implants, biologic solutions, spinal cord stimulation and brain modulation systems, implantable drug infusion systems, and interventional products, as well as nerve ablation system under the Accurian name. This segment offers its products for spinal surgeons, neurosurgeons, neurologists, pain management specialists, anesthesiologists, orthopedic surgeons, urologists, urogynecologists, and interventional radiologists, as well as ear, nose, and throat specialists, and energy surgical instruments. The Medical Surgical Portfolio segment offers surgical stapling devices, vessel sealing instruments, wound closure and electrosurgery products, AI-powered surgical video and analytics platform, robotic-assisted surgery products, hernia mechanical devices, mesh implants, gynecology products, gastrointestinal and hepatologic diagnostics and therapies, and therapies to treat diseases and conditions, and patient monitoring and airway management products, as well as insulin pumps and consumables, continuous glucose monitoring systems, and sensors. Medtronic plc was founded in 1949 and is headquartered in Galway, Ireland.
